# ++CHRCH Access

*Section: THE BOOK OF TRANSFORMATION: God Momentum*

- id: 27885125
- url: https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/27885125

## videos

- `iframe` https://dialog.filestackapi.com/dialog/comm_iframe/

- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/m-outer-3437aaddcdf6922d623e172c2d6f9278.html#url=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F27885125&title=7.%20The%20Five%20Stages%20Of%20Commitment%20(Babe%2C%20Passion%20Is%20Only%20%233)%20%7C%20The&referrer=&muid=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&sid=fdbd9602-a5fb-4729-889c-fc318f4a977cddf210&version=6&preview=false&__shared_params__[version]=v3

---

##    7\. The Five Stages Of Commitment (Babe, Passion Is Only #3) 

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/MrQomgtnTki3LZV4IhzL_passion2.JPG)

There are five stages of commitment.

'Interested' is stage #1

'Desire' is the second stage.

'Passion' is the third stage of commitment.

Ah, passion.

**Culture has us brainwashed that passion is everything.**

But you can't sustain passion.

It wasn't designed for that.

## Passion's role is to burn hot and quick. 

One moment you're passionate about something... The next moment you're passionate about something else. And on and on it goes.

The world is full of people who started out with passion.

Some kept the passion up for long enough to develop some skills. They turned those skills into a job. They did good. Then they died.

But no one remembers people who had passion.

They don't make statues of people who had passion.

The ones that end up standing out from the crowd? They've transcended that level.

They evolve to the FOURTH stage of commitment:

Obsession.

When you're obsessed, your goal is the first thing you think about when you wake up and the last thing you think about when you go to sleep.

The object of your obsession even appears in your dreams.

It's annoying sometimes, I admit.

This lifestyle is not for everybody.

That's why before you consider taking this road, ask yourself:

## _Are you sure you want that?  _

Yes, I know... All your heroes were obsessed.

They were willing to sacrifice their personal lives, their health, balanced meals, Game Of Thrones season finales and "cute relationships"... all in the pursuit of their goals. So that's why I have to ask you once again...

## _Are you sure you want that?_

You know you've found your "calling" when people think you've gone crazy.

To them, you are unpredictable. Unrecognizable.

They call you a genius but stop inviting you to the movies anymore because you'll probably derail the night into another one of your adventures.

That's why I urge you to think this through.

## **Are you sure you want that?  **

Leading a life of "passion" is so much easier!

It's more socially acceptable too.

You can be passionate and still go on ski trips with the family.

You can be passionate and still clock out 'cause it's "date night". 

But obsession is uncomfortable.

The sort of "uncomfortable" that has led to otherwise normal human beings do incredible things like finding cures to horrible diseases...

Or revolutionizing the state of music.

Or overthrowing whole governments.

Or leave behind 64,000 works of art. 

_But people don't like the word "obsession"._

It triggers them.

They prefer middle-of-the-road words like "moderation".

And then they quote articles from other semi-passionate people about work/life balance.

They say obsession is unhealthy behavior. 

But the way I see it.. Creatives out there are ALREADY obsessed.

They're just obsessed with all the wrong things.

Things that are destroying their lives.

Things that don't matter.

# For example, most creatives I meet are obsessed with "What other people think".

They've practically made it a religion.

I mean... Am I wrong?

That's why you reach for your phone every two minutes.

That's why you have anxiety.

And you think these obsessions are perfectly ok. And why? Because we live in a world where "everyone else is doing it".

Even if the reality is:

It's slowly killing you on the inside.

But the kind of obsession that I'm talking about, and want for you... Is way healthier than the bullshit they subscribe to.

I want you to direct your obsession towards "expressing your unique voice".

I want you to get so focused on your unique journey that you can no longer hear the negative influences that are crowding your headspace.

I want you to start being the total embodiment of your values and your beliefs.

Yes, I want you to have the kind of obsession that can SELF-GENERATE ENERGY for you to go on for hours… 

aka God Momentum

The kind of energy you exude when you know that what you're doing matters.

Obsession where every fiber of your being is dedicated to your mission.

Obsession where you see creativity in everything. And you act on it right away.

You've been operating at the passion level for your whole life.

Now it's time for you to evolve to a new level. 

Directing all your thoughts towards one thing.

Systematically and consciously.

When you focus on taking daily steps on a singular path... All the atoms of life start congregating in that direction to assist you. 

But you'll have to put energy out there consistently before you'll begin to see any results.

Don't be like a child who keeps unearthing the garden to see if the seeds are growing.

Be patient.

Have faith in the process, and in the laws of the universe.

They haven't failed anyone else who follows them.

So why would they fail you?

The reason people don't achieve their "goals"? Because they don't do what they "love" with obsession.

They do it with passion.

But they eventually find the repetition boring.

And then eventually find "being consistent" tiring.

So they do what they "love" with passion once a month and then wonder why nothing is changing.

# **Obsession "goes hard" even when it doesn't "feel like it".**

While you can be passionate about many things in life...

You can only be obsessed with one.

Passion affords you the luxury of time.

Obsession does not.

Passion is influenced by what "you're feeling that day". 

Obsession knows it's got a job to do either way.

Passion is that thing you talk about while you're doing something else.

Obsession is that thing you do and sorry you "don't have time to talk".

Passion is a temporary state of mind.

Obsession is a lifestyle.

When you find that every conversation you have and book you read relates back to your mission... 

You're obsessed.

When you're ready to work on your goal even on weekends, on birthdays, anniversaries and even Christmas...

You're obsessed.

The only people who will understand you?

Other obsessed people. 

So tell me...

Where do you fit in?

Those "goals" that you've been talking about for the past few months... 

The one's that you keep putting off, and putting off, and putting off again...

Are they your passion?

Or are they your obsession?

Oh, and yeah...

I almost forgot:

The FIFTH stage of commitment.

Yes, there is a stage more intense than obsession.

A rare altitude that's granted to only a few.

I include it here for the sake of completeness...

But it's probably not for you, no offense.

It's the closest seat to God.

The 5th stage of commitment is...

POSSESSION

It's when you're completely possessed by your purpose just like that girl in The Exorcist was by the devil.

You barf green lava on all your haters.

Family, friends try and fail to calm you down.

Institutions try to rid you of your demons because your attitude threatens the status quo.

But nothing can stop you.

Even mortality isn't an obstacle.

You know that you have a gift to return to humankind.

It's when your goal has captured YOU.

You have no choice in the matter anymore.

Your purpose is in your DNA. 

G.O.A.T.s are the only one's who operate at that level.

It's not a lifestyle you choose.

_It chooses you._

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

I think about my goals (dreams), the moment I wake-up, go to sleep and even during the day. I also get answers in my dreams, but my problem is (I don't know what to choose first because I have A LOT of different interests. I really need your help Father Bronques! I've also lost a lot of people, the moment I start focussing on me. I'm the weird one amongst my people. I would even work on birthdays, holidays.... normal people had a lot to say about it. It made me feel like I wasn't normal. I have an obsessive mind. So, that's why I'm very grateful for this course.

quick question what is the place for emotions and feelings regarding this process? All u said makes clear sense to me but it seems really pragmatic and logical. Isn't art all about expressing emotions and sharing it with the world? Should I use my emotions when doing my music or should I be very left brained during the process, I'm kinda lost regarding this and I want to be possessed but I'm scared that my art will lack any emotional substance. Or maybe should I work with my possessed demon and let my emotions decide if what I have done is good or not.... Answer me before I sell my soul to the devil Pls

nevermind... brain was being a pussy. Found the answer. Emotions are always and will always be regardless of if I'm disciplined or not. Discipline is only there if I take action. quote of the day by the future king : "Do it for yourself and yourself will do it."

I would add that while art is intrinsically emotional and almost volatile, the process of making it is probably the exact opposite of that. it takes that sort of logical and stoic and emotionless approach to be able to create art at a career level

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/27885125/comments/24040019)

When I understood obsession, everything but my art derailed. My ex-relationship ended. My friends left. My family thought I was going crazy. I was lonely for some time. I guess it paid off. 3 years into this journey, I have a registered brand, have done 2 exhibitions, 4 live sets, have a vault full of art that I keep sharing with my friends and fans, and found a group of people that became a second family to me that support me in any way they can. I wake up every morning with a goal and a way of living. Everything channels into my art. There are some nights I don't want to go to sleep because I feel there's something more I must do. It doesn't even feel bad. It feels like I have a purpose. Everything's about this symbol I created. The vessel for my art, my expression, my experiences, my message. Everything keeps evolving and getting better. It gives me something to look forward. The future is bright. I'll be on those billboards one day. On every roadblock, I kept going. No way I'm stopping now. Great fucking module, I had to reread this one out loud this time. IAMSVRREAL

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

Ashris Choudhury

now

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/27885125/comments/24042782)

This is what it's all about. You endured the hard part to get to the rewarding part. People always focus on the immediate discomfort, not realizing what world they would build for themselves if they stuck it out. I'd send you the link to the SECONDARY EFFECTS module... <https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/27885125> But, you don't need it 👏

![Denis Belousov](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4328731ad1dda49934eb37655e84234e?d=mm&s=70)

Denis Belousov

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/27885125/comments/16151915)

My obsession often harms my relationships, especially with my wife, I made my goals vocal many times in the past, and I don't want to sound rude, but I need to WORK ON MY SHIT cause nobody else will. Our most loved ones may not share that obsession, they have their own hopes, dreams, goals. Now that we have a beautiful little baby girl my time has been folded and cut in half, twice. Working a Full-time job from home doesn't even help, you'd think so much is taken out of the equation, but nope, your 'own time' has become a precious gift, never mind working on my craft. I want to be obsessed the way I am right now and still be a good father and husband, what can you suggest for busy parents, Father? Is there a healthy way of sharing my obsession with my wife, so I don't come out rude, selfish, and ignorant about the daily work I need to do?

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

Ashris Choudhury

now

![Father Bronques](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/6ab90ae46a200706e774b0b9fbeb0e8d?d=mm&s=70) Instructor 

Father Bronques

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/27885125/comments/16210119)

The only way I see that happening is if you make your family a part of your art, in subject matter AND in process as much as possible. The more boxes you have in your life, the more everything will suffer. Integrate everything so that it becomes fluid.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

I feel like this is something that also holds me back. I still want to have friends and family. Can you have relationships and still be obsessed?

![Father Bronques](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/6ab90ae46a200706e774b0b9fbeb0e8d?d=mm&s=70) Instructor 

Father Bronques

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/27885125/comments/18357147)

Of course you can have relationships and be obsessed. But for those relationships not to suffer, the people you're in a relationship with have to understand that they're #2 not #1. If that doesn't sound appealing to you, you're not obsessed yet. Obsessed wouldn't even think to ask me this question (#truthlotion).

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

![Karolina Parysz](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/5c2b4f9e0e91342ade5b5d74ddf35c3f?d=mm&s=70)

Karolina Parysz

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/27885125/comments/18377751)

Oh, no, damn no. I can not agree here. It's #truthlotion only for someone who haven't found the right person yet or indeed is NOT a parent and partner. Sorry Father but this is shit advice for someone responsible for another human being and WANTING to be a good parent and partner. If obsession will come #1st and this is how you want to raise your daughter – with narrative that something is always more important than her, her needs, that she's not worthy spending time with her father – better start saving for the therapist already. Trust me, I've been there. Family can and should come as equals. This path doesn't have to be lonely and doesn't have to make people around you suffer in order to connect and help others. When having respectful, true partnership, you absolutely CAN come up with right system, goals, boundaries and processes to make it ALL work. Just keep on talking, explaining, having calm, long conversations, put time and effort to make it work and it will work. But if you simply turn your back on people that you love, that love you, that you're already connected to, to "work on your shit" you're setting yourself up for lonely and sad, sad life with burnt bridges and regrets behind you.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

Ashris Choudhury

![Father Bronques](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/6ab90ae46a200706e774b0b9fbeb0e8d?d=mm&s=70) Instructor 

Father Bronques

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/27885125/comments/18378694)

If someone finds a way of "making it all work" that's beautiful. But that's called balance. Not obsession. Obsession is lopsided by nature. So, I agree... If your #1 goal is to be the best parent and partner, then you should NOT be obsessed with your art. You should be obsessed with raising a great home. But, if you choose to make art your obsession (your #1), then get all the people in your life on the same page for it to work. Let them know that they will be #2 for a while. Only YOU can choose which of those directions are right for you. But sorry... Everything can't be number one-- The attempt to do that is called "average".

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

Ashris Choudhury
